title: Intro to Cloud Storage
subtitle: Save, Share & Access Files Anywhere!
overview: This session introduces students to cloud storage and how files can be saved online instead of only on a computer or mobile device. Students will learn about Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, file sharing, online safety, and teamwork using cloud technology.

images._note: Add photos from the classroom session — students using cloud storage, screenshots of Google Drive, uploading files, teamwork activities, and internet concepts.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Illustration of cloud storage concept
gallery.caption: Cloud Storage lets us save files online safely
Google Drive helps users save and share files online

gallery.caption: Students learning how to upload files online

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Image showing same files on laptop and mobile
gallery.caption: Cloud storage syncs files across devices
Students collaborating using shared cloud documents

gallery.caption: Strong passwords keep cloud accounts safe

gallery.caption: Popular cloud services: Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox

keyTopics: What is Cloud Storage? — Saving files on the internet
Access files from mobile, tablet, or computer
Benefits of Cloud Storage — Backup, Sharing, Teamwork

activity.title: Upload and Share a File Using Cloud Storage
steps: Create a new folder and move the file inside
Share the file link with teacher or classmate

quiz.explanation: Cloud storage means saving files online so they can be accessed anytime using the internet.

quiz.q: Which is an example of cloud storage?
quiz.explanation: Google Drive is a popular cloud storage platform.

quiz.q: What do you need to access cloud files?
quiz.explanation: Cloud storage works using the internet.

quiz.q: What is the benefit of cloud storage?
quiz.explanation: Cloud storage allows users to access files from different devices and places.

quiz.q: Which action sends a file to cloud storage?
quiz.explanation: Uploading means sending files from your device to cloud storage.

options: Saving online files to your device
quiz.explanation: Downloading means saving files from the cloud onto your device.

quiz.q: Why should we use strong passwords?
options: To protect our account and files
quiz.explanation: Strong passwords help keep cloud accounts safe from hackers.

quiz.q: Which device can access cloud storage?
quiz.explanation: Cloud storage can be used on computers, mobiles, and tablets.

options: Sending file access to another person
quiz.explanation: File sharing allows others to view or edit your files online.
Google Drive is a cloud storage service developed by Google.

quiz.q: What helps organize files in cloud storage?
quiz.explanation: Folders help keep files organized neatly.

quiz.q: What happens if your computer is damaged but files are in cloud storage?
quiz.explanation: Cloud storage keeps files safe online even if your device is damaged.

quiz.q: Which feature allows teamwork on the same document?
quiz.explanation: Cloud tools allow multiple users to work together on the same document.

quiz.q: What icon is commonly used to represent cloud storage?
quiz.explanation: Cloud services are usually represented with a cloud symbol.

quiz.q: What is the main idea of cloud storage?
quiz.explanation: Cloud storage helps users safely save and manage files online.
